title: hulsea vestita subsp. inyoensis
intro: Hulsea vestita subsp. inyoensis, also known as Inyo hulsea or Inyo mountain hulsea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is a perennial herb native to the Inyo Mountains in California, United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties and is believed to possess medicinal properties that can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. Inyo hulsea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and reduce stress.
